message:
fix(math_abi): improve C23 log/exp compound functions and totalorder

Fix numerical precision issues in several C23 math ABI functions by
using mathematically equivalent formulations that avoid catastrophic
cancellation near zero:

log2p1/log2p1f: Replace log2(1+x) with log1p(x)/LN_2. The naive
  formulation loses precision when x is near zero because 1+x rounds
  away the small x contribution before log2 sees it. log1p is
  specifically designed to handle this case accurately.

log10p1/log10p1f: Same fix, using log1p(x)/LN_10 instead of
  log10(1+x).

exp2m1/exp2m1f: Replace exp2(x)-1 with expm1(x*LN_2). The naive
  subtraction loses precision when x is near zero because exp2(x)
  is near 1.0 and the subtraction cancels most significant bits.

exp10m1/exp10m1f: Same fix, using expm1(x*LN_10) instead of
  exp10(x)-1.

rootn/rootnf: Add n==0 guard returning NAN. Previously, rootn(x, 0)
  would compute pow(x, 1/0) = pow(x, inf) which is mathematically
  undefined but would silently return 0, 1, or inf depending on x.
  IEEE 754-2019 specifies rootn(x, 0) = NAN.

totalorder/totalorderf: Fix the sign-magnitude to monotonic ordering
  conversion. The previous implementation used !ai (bitwise NOT) for
  negative values and ai^(1<<63) for positive values, which produced
  an incorrect ordering for mixed-sign comparisons. The correct
  transformation is: negative floats get magnitude bits flipped
  (ai ^ MAX), positive floats are left as-is. This matches the
  IEEE 754-2019 totalOrder predicate specification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
